programmes.show_description: Moving Target: The hugely popular crime drama series with a cast of the nation's favourites. The UCOS team reinvestigate a hit and run that left a cycle courier with amnesia.
Motherland: When two small children are left in Miep and Jan's care, it strengthens Jan's ties to the resistance and brings up Miep's issues with her past abandonment.
Confidence: Chigwell comedy with the two sisters and their irrepressibly naughty neighbour. Dorien joins the Open University, and Sharon perseveres with her job at the bakery.
Sparring with Death: Final Chapter: A second death at the Wildemarsh Wellness Retreat leads Mattie to uncover a heart-wrenching tragedy, waiting to be avenged.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Thug on the Tyne: Part 2: After Rawton's cover seems to have been blown, Burnside finds himself in deep water.
Riot City: Conway mans the barricades in a riot situation, but Glaze could find himself facing the real thing.
Crime and Punishment: Hollis stumbles across some grave goings-on at a crematorium, while Brownlow confronts a ghost from his past.
Zero Tolerance: Inspector Monroe clamps down on anti-social behaviour among the youth of Sun Hill, but others suffer as a result of his single-mindedness.
Scoop: Conway becomes involved with a call girl, thanks to Boulton. But is the charming Linda all that she seems?
Nurse Crane and Trixie investigate when a mother abandons her children at the clinic. When the woman is sent to a neurologist, the results are devastating for the whole family.
Sister Julienne helps a woman from Pakistan who is unable to have children, but who has found to her shock that her husband is bringing a pregnant second wife into their home.

programmes.show_name: Catherine Cookson: A Dinner of Herbs
programmes.show_description: First in a gripping two-part drama adapted from Cookson's best-selling book in which a sailor's plans to start a new life with his son come to a tragic end.
Scoop: The body of a high-level government official's secretary is found at the bottom of a staircase - and Sister Boniface claims she was dead before the fall.
Look to the Lady - Part Two: The adventure continues for our quirky 1930s detective, memorably played by Peter Davison. Campion travels to Suffolk to protect a medieval chalice.
For Whom the Bell Tolls: Harry and Fergus travel to the convent of a silent order of nuns when a friend asks Harry to investigate a suspicious suicide, believing foul play is afoot.
River's Edge: Emilia Fox stars in a feature-length forensics mystery. The Lyell team investigate the shooting of a family who were innocently picnicking by a river.
Communal Living: More adventures with the ageing team of sleuths. As Brian struggles to control and conceal his desire to drink again, he finds the perfect refuge in a commune.
Nurse Crane and Trixie investigate when a mother abandons her children at the clinic. When the woman is sent to a neurologist, the results are devastating for the whole family.
The Butterfly: Miep must choose between her morals and her best friend, and discovers her husband Jan is deliberately lying to her - but is he doing it out of love?
Final Bid: Mick and Shazia investigate the theft of a rare Chinese vase which has disappeared from the locked vault of a Mayfair auction house.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
The Front: Comedy series that proves there's more to Essex girls than white stilettos. Dorien's life story is about to go into print, but she suddenly gets cold feet.
Beasts: Sam Harker finds his loyalties put to the test when he investigates a dangerous group of animal liberation activists.
New. A Kind of Hush: Vivien does not turn up for her driving test, so Jimmi goes to check on her - only to find she has been burgled and raped.
New. Mummy Dearest: Vivien refuses to let Jimmi help her after the break-in at her home, while Ronnie and Nick have to deal with a tricky custody problem.
A Knight's Tale: Zubin's jealousy leaves Jess wondering if she's made a mistake with Reza. Connie is determined to show Will who's in charge, while Tricia thinks she's driven Carlos away.
Til Death Do Us Part: Warren wakes from his coma and confronts Adam about his affair with Kirsty, while Jordan clashes with the new joint clinical lead, who is his former mentor.
Beasts: Sam Harker finds his loyalties put to the test when he investigates a dangerous group of animal liberation activists.
New. Ronnie tries to protect Jack from the truth. Fatboy has an extreme plan to get Leon back in Zsa Zsa's good books and Lucy's secret is finally out.
New. Ronnie discovers the real cost of the fire, whilst Billy keeps a low profile. Zainab cruelly re-writes history and Ian tries to get Jane off the Square.
Mending Fences: The acclaimed character actor Robert Hardy stars in the popular vet drama. A birthday celebration is hit by tragedy. The vets feel the long arm of the law.
Laura and David spend the night together, and Charles refuses to attend his father's funeral.
A High Profile: Look out for Dominic Monaghan, 'Merry' in the Lord of the Rings trilogy, here in an early role in the gentle crime drama. Hetty takes on a case for a worried mother.
Confidence: Chigwell comedy with the two sisters and their irrepressibly naughty neighbour. Dorien joins the Open University, and Sharon perseveres with her job at the bakery.
The Flag and Its Snags: A trip to a land of ferrets, wrinkly stockings and cloth caps in the much-loved sitcom with a fine cast. Foggy wants to plant a flag in British soil.
The Flag and Further Snags: Fun and a dash of naughtiness from the three indomitable old codgers. Will one of Foggy's mad schemes, this time involving a flag, finally bear fruit?
Deep in the Heart of Yorkshire: Ferrets, wrinkly stockings, and Compo being chased by Nora Batty with a broom. Sid and Wally's wives are suspicious of their other halves.
Playing the Lie: Neill Rea stars in the crime drama set in the fictitious town of Brokenwood in New Zealand. DI Shepherd investigates when the owner of a golf club drops dead.
Communal Living: More adventures with the ageing team of sleuths. As Brian struggles to control and conceal his desire to drink again, he finds the perfect refuge in a commune.
An Advancement of Learning: Warren Clarke stars as the detective duo tackle another mystery. A shocking find is made when a memorial to the former principal of a college is taken.
